countries.name: DR Congo
health_zones: Tchomia (NEW — Lake Albert shore, ~50km S of Bunia)

updates.text: Reuters: 635 confirmed / 127 deaths / 30 recoveries — 37 new cases + 12 deaths in 24 hours, all in Ituri; NEW health zone: Tchomia (Lake Albert shore, ~50km south of Bunia); 26 zones nationwide / 18 in Ituri (94%+ of cases); treatment centres active in Bunia + Rwampara

CIDRAP/WHO: 598 confirmed / 115 deaths / 297 in treatment / 22 recovered — CFR 19.2%; health zones rose from 16 to 25 since June 1; WHO rapid risk assessment: DRC very high, Uganda + neighbours high; Trump admin requests European travel restrictions for World Cup (begins June 11) — Belgium refuses, EC: 'no evidence additional measures needed'

Lancet Infectious Diseases: outbreak could be as large as 1,354 cases (two scenario models: CFR-33% model → 1,164 cases by May 27; travel/population model → 1,354 by May 27) — 'considerable knowledge gaps' and 'possibly substantially undetected transmission'

Red Cross UK: 550 confirmed cases / 101 confirmed deaths / 94 suspected cases — Rwampara and Mongwalu (dense mining communities) remain the two hardest-hit areas; Uganda: 19 confirmed cases / 2 suspected deaths

CDC: 4 designated US airports for Ebola screening — IAD (May 20), ATL (May 22), IAH (May 26), JFK (May 28); HHS interim final rule extends entry suspension to lawful permanent residents (LPRs) under 42 CFR Part 71.40

DRC context: 44 attacks on healthcare facilities in DRC since January 2025; previous Bundibugyo outbreaks (2007, 2012) lasted ~97–106 days and were contained without vaccine — current outbreak has compounding factors (multi-province, no vaccine, 4-week detection lag, active conflict)

WHO DON (as of June 6): 534 total confirmed / 93 deaths (CFR 17.4%) — DRC 515 confirmed / 91 deaths; Uganda 19 confirmed / 2 deaths + 1 probable death; 17 total recoveries; 25 health zones affected across Ituri, North Kivu, South Kivu
DRC contact tracing rates: Ituri 43.2%, North Kivu 82.5%, South Kivu 80.3% — 5,040 contacts under follow-up; Ituri CFR 15% (74/487 cases); North Kivu CFR 64% (16/25 cases)
Uganda case detail: 70% of cases are Congolese nationals seeking medical care; 14 imported / 5 Ugandan nationals; districts: Kampala & Wakiso; no documented community transmission
UAE CASE: Congolese national traveled DRC→Uganda→UAE→Uganda; UAE conducted rapid contact tracing — no secondary cases, no local transmission; risk in UAE assessed as LOW
WHO risk reassessment: DRC = VERY HIGH (ongoing spread to new health zones); Uganda = HIGH; countries sharing land borders with DRC/Uganda = HIGH; rest of Africa + global = LOW
Africa CDC + WHO launch joint continental preparedness and response plan — $518M requested to support African countries in detection and response
DRC: 515 confirmed cases / 91 confirmed deaths — 27 new samples tested positive in previous 24 hours (Reuters / DRC government data)
Air France Flight 378 Paris→Detroit diverted to Montreal after Congolese passenger boarded in error
DHS requires all DRC/Uganda/South Sudan travelers to enter US via Dulles (IAD) only
WHO: vaccine 6–9 months from clinical trials; patient zero still not identified
Africa CDC: 282 confirmed / 43 confirmed deaths / 1,100+ suspected / 246 suspected deaths; Ituri: 264 of 282 confirmed; DRC/Uganda/South Sudan adopt $319M response plan
Contact tracing now 45% (up from 7%); all 5 survivors are health workers (4 nurses + 1 lab worker)

Hundreds protest at Laikipia Air Base gates; Kenya court suspension still in effect; Italy: patient from Congo tests NEGATIVE; Brazil: 2 patients — meningitis and malaria — Ebola not formally ruled out
Brazil: suspected Ebola case in São Paulo — 37yo man returned from DRC, in isolation at infectious diseases institute
5 total recoveries; 4 discharged at new Bunia treatment center opening; WHO: 134 confirmed / 18 confirmed deaths; nurse tested 7 times before confirmed
MSF: 'Never before has an Ebola outbreak recorded so many cases so soon after declaration' — deeply alarming; hundreds of samples untested; Uganda 9 confirmed
New treatment center opened in Bunia; local lab now returns results in 24 hours (was 1,500km to Kinshasa); ADF killed 7 in Beni, North Kivu
WHO: current CFR 30-50% based on confirmed cases — Legand: 'up to 5 out of 10 people likely to die'; Uganda now 9 confirmed cases; Tedros: travel bans 'don't help much'

Kenya facility: Laikipia Air Base, opens Friday, 50 beds; 30+ US Public Health staff with 3 days training; Americans who test positive going to unidentified European countries; Kenya medical union 48-hour strike threat
Rubio: $112M pledged to regional response + $13.5M to Kenya; WHO: 4.6 tonnes of aid at Bunia; UNICEF sending 100 tonnes; 245,000+ fled eastern DRC since Jan 2025
FUNDING HALVED: pledges for Ebola response fell sharply since start of week — Africa CDC; previously $500M announced
5 schoolchildren killed by Ebola — DRC refuses to close schools in Ituri; deaths linked to self-medication and delayed care
NICD sitrep (May 27): CFR 22.8% suspected / 14% confirmed; 4 confirmed deaths are healthcare workers; 13 health zones; Ituri 110 conf / North Kivu 10 conf / South Kivu 1 conf

KENYA HIGH COURT BLOCKED US EBOLA QUARANTINE FACILITY — Katiba Institute petition; court bars any foreign government; prohibits admission of anyone exposed; US planned 50-bed facility expandable to 250
FIRST PATIENT RECOVERED — discharged May 28 after 2 negative tests; CDC (May 29): 1,077 suspected / 121 confirmed / 246 suspected deaths / 17 confirmed deaths
Africa CDC: vaccine expected by end of 2026; 105 in treatment centers; Bunia camp Kingonze: 9 people in 3 square meters — 'If a single person gets infected here, everyone will die'
1,000+ suspected / 246 deaths; Rwanda also closed its border with DRC; $500M pledged by international donors; Rubio: 'cannot and will not allow any cases of Ebola to enter the United States'

Canada's 90-day ban was NOT based on public health advice — driven by FIFA World Cup; WHO advises against travel restrictions; Africa CDC Dr. Kaseya: 'shame that South Sudan — zero cases — is under travel restriction'
WHO treatment candidates for trials: Mapp antibody, Regeneron (Inmazeb component), remdesivir, obeldesivir (post-exposure); Oxford/Serum vaccine: 2-3 months to trial
Deaths now 246; global health funding lowest since 2009; only 1 in 5 contacts traced — Dr. Gounder (KFF): 'each of those 4 out of 5 could set off a new chain of transmission'
US will NOT bring Ebola patients home — Americans sent to Europe or Kenya quarantine facility; Trump on record in 2014 against Ebola repatriation; CDC staff asked to volunteer for Kenya unit
World Cup in Houston in <3 weeks — 'we do not have a health preparedness plan in place' — Dr. Gounder; DRC team in Belgium for months (not a risk)

DRC suspended all Bunia flights (humanitarian/medical exceptions allowed); Jordan & Bahrain: entry bans since May 19; India: screening + postponed India-Africa summit; Thailand: DRC/Uganda visitors via Bangkok only after negative test; Mexico: increased airport screening
CDC (May 26): 105 confirmed / 906 suspected / 223 suspected deaths / 10 confirmed deaths in Congo; Uganda: 7 confirmed / 1 confirmed death
Title 42 invoked: bars immigrants AND legal permanent residents (LPRs) who were in DRC/Uganda/South Sudan in prior 21 days; may also block exposed US citizens from returning
Bunia displacement camp: 1 handwashing station and 1 thermometer for 10,000 people; those without soap wash hands with oatmeal or sand

Uganda closes DRC border 'with immediate effect' — against WHO guidance; WHO warns closures push people to unmonitored footpaths; exceptions only for outbreak response, cargo, security; all entrants: 21-day mandatory isolation
Only 7% of known contacts traced; WHO internal doc: 'No vaccine. No therapy. Undetected 6 weeks. HCWs dying. Every day without a fully resourced response is a day the outbreak gains ground.'
Tedros arrived DRC; called for immediate ceasefire: 'catastrophic collision of disease and conflict'; 'cannot build community trust while bombs are falling'
Canada: 90-day entry ban (DRC/Uganda/South Sudan); Bahamas: quarantine rules; US ban already in effect for non-citizens
Of 220 suspected deaths, only 17 lab-confirmed; ~1,000 showing symptoms; 3,600 contacts identified; experimental US antibody treatment may be introduced soon; ECDC/EU Health Task Force deploying
Tedros flying to DRC Tuesday with WHO emergencies chief Chikwe Ihekweazu; told African Union: epidemic 'likely to get worse before it gets better'
Uganda halts all flights to/from DRC; restricts land border crossings; outbreak now covers area larger than Florida

PATIENT ZERO: Nurse, symptoms April 24 in Bunia, buried in Mongbwalu; Mongbwalu had 4 health workers die in single week in April before Ebola was identified; widespread panic blamed on supernatural causes
3RD ATTACK: Armed men stormed Mongbwalu General Hospital with gunfire demanding bodies — staff evacuated patients under fire; Hospital on general alert
904+ suspected / 220+ deaths / 100+ confirmed / Uganda 7 cases; 3rd largest Ebola outbreak on record just 10 days after declaration; US intel: virus highly likely already in South Sudan

Bunia lab: ~40 tests/day; one day only 20 — generator ran out of fuel; hand sanitizer sold out in all pharmacies; IRC found empty PPE shelves at outbreak declaration: 'Zero. Everyone was totally unprepared'

HOW IT SPREAD: Patient-zero died in Bunia; family transported body 80km to Mongbwalu; bumpy roads cracked coffin, exposing corpse; provincial lab missed Ebola (wrong strain tested); samples sent 1,800km to Kinshasa to confirm
Mongbwalu alone: 322 suspected cases / 88 deaths; ~100km from Uganda, ~200km from South Sudan; gold miners and traders criss-cross region daily
TOLL TOPS 200: DRC health ministry confirms 204 deaths / 867 suspected cases — DRC's 17th Ebola outbreak
Uganda: 3 new cases — total now 5 confirmed / 1 dead; includes driver, health worker, and DRC woman who traveled Arua→Entebbe before testing positive
Africa CDC: 10 countries now at risk — Angola, Burundi, CAR, Rep. of Congo, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, Zambia
CRITICAL: MSF Ebola treatment tent torched in Mongbwalu — 18 suspected cases fled into community, now unaccounted for (2nd attack in a week)
WHO upgrades DRC risk from HIGH → VERY HIGH; Tedros: outbreak 'much larger' than 82 confirmed cases suggest
OUTBREAK EARLIER THAN THOUGHT: 3 Red Cross volunteers believed infected March 27 — nearly a month before first known death
ActionAid: nearly 1 in 3 schools in Bunia, Nizi and Nyankunde have suspected Ebola case or close contact
Ituri bans funeral wakes; burials by specialized teams only; gatherings capped at 50 people
UN releases $60M CERF emergency fund; US pledges $23M + 50 treatment clinics
IS-linked militants killed 17 in Alima village, Ituri — armed conflict compounding response
WHO: Uganda no onwards transmission among contacts; both confirmed cases linked to DRC
WARNING: GeneXpert platform CANNOT detect Bundibugyo virus — WHO advisory to field teams
NEW PROVINCE: South Kivu confirms first Ebola case — 28yo death near Bukavu in M23 rebel-held area
Funding cuts forced suspension of surveillance in multiple DRC areas; 920K+ IDPs in Ituri
Canada (Ontario): 1 person hospitalized and tested for Ebola after travel to East Africa — NOT confirmed
WaPo: White House resisted Stafford evacuation to US, delaying care (WH denies)
US missionary Peter Stafford airlifted to Berlin Charite Hospital for treatment
6 high-risk US contacts traveling to Germany and Czech Republic for monitoring
US State Dept funding 50 treatment clinics in DRC and Uganda via CERF/OCHA
WHO declares PHEIC — Bundibugyo strain, DRC and Uganda
US CDC imposes travel restrictions for DRC, Uganda, South Sudan (21-day)
